body:
Title: Revenu Quebec Schedule C 2025 tax credit for childcare expenses.

Source:
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/en/online-services/forms-and-publications/current-details/tp-1-v/
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/documents/en/formulaires/tp/2025-12/TP-1.D.C-V%282025-12%29.pdf

Schedule C numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 13658
Value: 16800
Value: 12275
Value: 6180
Value: 24795
Value: 43725
Value: 45340
Value: 46970
Value: 48570
Value: 50195
Value: 119835
Value: 0.78
Value: 0.75
Value: 0.74
Value: 0.73
Value: 0.72
Value: 0.71
Value: 0.70
Value: 0.67
Value: 78
Value: 75
Value: 74
Value: 73
Value: 72
Value: 71
Value: 70
Value: 67

Schedule C defines an eligible child as a child under 16 at some point in the year or a child of any age with a mental or physical infirmity. A dependant child can be eligible if the child's income for the year was not more than $13,658. Part A line 39 adds childcare expenses on lines 26.1 through 38.1. Line 40 subtracts allowances or reimbursements for childcare expenses shown on RL slips if the expenses are included on line 39. Line 41 gives childcare expenses qualifying for the tax credit.

Part B calculates the childcare expense limit. Line 43 multiplies the number of eligible children with a severe and prolonged impairment by $16,800. Line 45 multiplies the number of eligible children born after December 31, 2018 by $12,275. Line 47 multiplies the number of eligible children born after December 31, 2008 or with an infirmity by $6,180. Line 50 adds lines 43, 45, and 47. Part C line 80 adds the taxpayer's and spouse's line 275 net income for family income.

Part D line 85 is the lesser of line 41 and line 50. The tax credit rate table assigns 78% if family income is from $0 to $24,795, 75% if more than $24,795 and not more than $43,725, 74% if more than $43,725 and not more than $45,340, 73% if more than $45,340 and not more than $46,970, 72% if more than $46,970 and not more than $48,570, 71% if more than $48,570 and not more than $50,195, 70% if more than $50,195 and not more than $119,835, and 67% if more than $119,835. Line 94 multiplies line 85 by the tax credit rate on line 92. Line 98 subtracts the amount claimed by the spouse on line 455 and carries the result to TP-1 line 455.
